name: Dfina (Sabbath Stew)
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds beef (flank steak, short ribs or brisket), cut into 2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the chickpeas and navy beans in separate bowls. Add enough water to each bowl to just cover and soak overnight.
2. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F. In a medium skillet, heat oil over medium high heat and saute onions until golden brown.
3. Place potatoes, onions, meat (in their shells), and eggs (in their shells) into a large ovenproof pot or casserole with a tight fitting lid. Be careful not to break the eggs.
4. Drain chickpeas and beans, discarding soaking liquid, and add them to the pot. Add garlic, coriander, cumin, and allspice; mix well.
5. Add enough water to the pot to barely cover contents. If the lid of the pot does not fit tightly, put a layer of foil between the pot and lid.
6. Bake stew for 1 hour.
7. Stir in salt and pepper. Reduce heat to lowest setting and cook for an additional 5 to 8 hours.
8. Remove eggs from shells and cut into quarters. Serve stew hot, garnished with egg.
This version is from Egypt and is characterized by the hard-cooked eggs which have delicious, creamy yolks from cooking so slowly.
Cooking the Israeli Way by Josephine Bacon p. 38
Lerner Publications Co. 1986 ISBN: 0-8225-0912-1
